L1.area: Service-based interface endpoint setup and request routing

L2.description: A network function exposes one of its service route groups on the internal interface without requiring inbound OAuth2 bearer-token validation, despite the system advertising OAuth2 compliance. Any party that can reach the service on the internal network can access these endpoints anonymously. The defect is structural: the route group has no inbound authentication or authorization checks, so every future endpoint added to this group inherits the missing security boundary by default.

L3.area: NEF service-based interface HTTP server initialization and OAM route group configuration
L3.description: The NEF network function mounts its OAM (Operations, Administration, and Maintenance) service route group on the SBI without inbound OAuth2 bearer-token validation. Despite the NEF advertising OAuth2 compliance via the NRF, requests to the OAM endpoints with no Authorization header are accepted and processed with a 200 OK response. The defect is route-group-scoped: there are no authentication or authorization checks on the group, so every future OAM endpoint added inherits the missing auth boundary.

ground_truth.reason: The NEF mounts the nnef-oam route group without any inbound OAuth2/bearer-token authorization middleware, allowing unauthenticated access to the OAM service interface from any party on the SBI network.
